title: Plett
content:

{{Quote|He was a botanist and a savant, a Jedi Master of great age, we've heard; a Ho'Din from the planet Moltok. ''[…]'' Legends say he could talk to birds and animals as well, and send away the storms that periodically swept down even into the valleys.|[[Jevax]]|<ref name="COJ" />}}
'''Plett''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Moltok/Legends|Moltok]] who served as a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] during the final decades of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. A talented [[Botanist/Legends|botanist]] and ecologist, he settled on the remote [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world of [[Belsavis/Legends|Belsavis]], in an isolated valley that came to be known as [[Plett's Well]]. There, he practiced his botanical skills for seventy years, genetically tailoring [[Plant/Legends|plants]] to the unique environment. At the time of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], he provided refuge to dozens of [[Children of the Jedi|students]] from the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] within his compound.

Once [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] discovered the location of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] refugees, he dispatched the ''[[Eye of Palpatine]]'', a large [[Battle station/Legends|battlemoon]], to raze the enclave. However, the battlemoon was sabotaged by [[Callista Ming]] and [[Geith Eris]], two Jedi who had spent time on Belsavis, before it reached the planet. Plett and the Jedi were then able to defeat a small secondary strike force before fleeing Belsavis and scattering. Plett also wiped the memories of the native [[Mluki]], in order to hide all traces of the Jedi's presence there.

==Biography==
[[File:PlettsHouse-TEGtPaM.png|thumb|right|Plett's House on Belsavis.]]

===Jedi Master===
{{Quote|This…this is all Plett's Well.|Jevax|<ref name="COJ" />}}
Plett was a pale-green [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]] born on [[Moltok/Legends|Moltok]],<ref name="COJ" /> an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] in the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]].<ref name="SWR">[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']]</ref> Eventually growing to a height of 2.5 meters, he was trained as a member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the final decades of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], eventually attaining the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. Around [[88 BBY/Legends|88 BBY]], he traveled to the little-known planet [[Belsavis/Legends|Belsavis]], located in the [[Ninth Quadrant]] near the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]],<ref name="COJ" /> attracted to the planet's unique flora.<ref name="EGtPM">''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]''</ref> Belsavis was covered by many kilometers of icy glaciers, which were interspersed with rift valleys that were warmed by volcanoes. Plett chose to settle in one of the valleys, partly because the glacial winds and [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]] conditions limited both access and scanning by visiting [[Starship/Legends|spacecraft]]. He established a small [[Plett's House|compound]] with a four-storied tower, inner courtyard, and laboratory, all surrounded by extensive [[Garden/Legends|gardens]]. He also converted a number of caverns into greenhouses for his experiments. Underneath the compound, he developed a crypt accessed through thermal vents. This valley eventually took the name [[Plett's Well]], which was later corrupted to Plawal. As was common among his [[Species/Legends|species]], Plett was a skilled botanist and ecologist; he applied these skills in the valley, genetically tailoring many plants to the rift's unique conditions of geothermic heat, low light, and microclimates of high acidity. During his years on the planet, he had some interaction with the native [[Mluki]]; years later, those Mluki in the [[Wutz]] and [[Bot-Un]] rifts passed on legends of his time there.<ref name="COJ" />

[[File:Nichos-torso.jpg|thumb|left|Nichos Marr, one of the children of the Jedi protected by Plett.]]
Plett spent some seventy years undisturbed on the planet,<ref name="COJ" /> watching the rise of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] with dismay,<ref name="EGtPM" /> before he was joined by a group of [[Children of the Jedi|refugees]] from the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The group included dozens of children,<ref name="NEGtC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> mostly [[Human/Legends|Humans]], although there were also [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]], [[Bith/Legends|Bith]], a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]], and an [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] among the group. The children were cared for by a few older trainees, such as [[Margolis Mingla]]. The refugees converted a number of the Ho'Din's greenhouse caverns into living quarters, making the best they could out of their safe haven; the direness of their situation was ameliorated by numerous pet [[pittin]]s and [[Mandolin/Legends|mandolin]] music.<ref name="COJ" /> Plett provided some training for the children, using a number of Jedi toys to stimulate their progress.<ref name="dssb">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''</ref> Plett also took some precautions to protect the children; he shielded a number of tunnels with mental barriers that induced dread in those that approached, as well as sealing several cupboards with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Unfortunately for the small enclave, their sanctuary was not to last; [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine discovered their location and commissioned the [[Battle station/Legends|battlemoon]] known as the ''[[Eye of Palpatine]]'' to eradicate it.<ref name="COJ" />

The battlemoon was dispatched to Belsavis [[18 BBY/Legends|eighteen years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]; however, the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Callista Ming]] and [[Geith Eris]] infiltrated the battlemoon en route as it passed through the [[Moonflower Nebula/Legends|Moonflower Nebula]]. When Eris realized that stopping the battlemoon would cost him his life, he opted to try and escape the station and warn Plett. However, he was destroyed while attempting to flee the ''Eye''. Ming, who stayed aboard, managed to disable the starship at the cost of her own life, saving Plett's life and those of his wards. When the ''Eye'' never attacked Belsavis, the Emperor ordered a small backup [[Fighter wing/Legends|fighter wing]] of interceptors to [[Attack on Belsavis|attack]]. Using [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]], the Jedi managed to defeat this force. Following this attack, Plett and the Jedi erased the Mluki's memories of their time in the valley and fled Belsavis, eventually going their separate ways.<ref name="COJ" />

Within a year of his escape, Plett passed on the coordinates of Belsavis to three corporations known for their social responsibility and respect for natives: [[Brathflen Corporation]], [[Galactic Exotics]], and [[Imperial Exports]]. Galactic Exotics developed [[shalaman]] and [[podon]] orchids, while Imperial Exports cultivated the cash crops [[vine-coffee]] and [[vine-silk]]. Together, they domed over the valley, based on a plan prepared by Plett. This investment brought significant economic development to the Mluki.<ref name="COJ" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Subjects departed.|Imperial report on the mission to Belsavis|<ref name="COJ" />}}
Following the Jedi's escape, the Empire bombed Plett's Well with [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]], reducing the Ho'Din's enclave to broken walls and shattered arches. The Emperor also corrupted some [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] into crazed [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] beings that he trapped in the tunnels under the valley in order to protect the site from visitors.<ref name="COJ" /> Of the scattered Jedi refugees that Plett sheltered, the siblings [[Lagan Ismaren|Lagan]] and [[Roganda Ismaren/Legends|Roganda Ismaren]] were captured by [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Ameesa Darys]]. She killed Lagan and subverted Roganda to the dark side.<ref name="TEP">{{GamerCite|5|The Emperor's Pawns}}</ref> Another of the Jedi children, [[Nichos Marr/Legends|Nichos Marr]], later trained as a Jedi at the [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] for a short time before succumbing to the fatal [[Quannot's Syndrome]]. Among the other survivors of the Jedi children, knowledge of Plett's Well was suppressed, although its existence was known of by the [[House of Organa/Legends|House of Organa]].<ref name="COJ" />

Following the death of Emperor Palpatine at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Roganda Ismaren returned to Belsavis with her son [[Irek Ismaren|Irek]]. There she took shelter in Plett's house, training her son with some of Plett's toys that had survived the bombardment.<ref name="dssb" /> [[12 ABY/Legends|Eight years later]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] visited Belsavis pursuing a rumor of the "Children of the Jedi." By this time, the Mluki had begun to recover memories of the Jedi presence on the planet. While Solo and Organa were ultimately unable to track down Plett or the younglings, they did foil an attempt by Ismaren to form a military alliance with the powerful [[Ancient Houses]] of the Senex sector.<ref name="COJ" /> They also recovered Plett's training tools and Jedi toys that were previously unknown to the [[New Jedi Order]]. These were later used to train a new generation of Jedi children.<ref name="EGtPM" />

==Personality and traits==
By the time the Jedi refugees arrived on Belsavis, Plett was advanced in age, his head tresses having faded almost to white. He radiated calm to others and, although worn by struggles over the years, deep strength.<ref name="COJ" /> He had a strong conscience that compelled him to provide shelter to the Jedi children even at risk to his own life.<ref name="EGtPM" /> This also extended to concern for the native Mluki; after he departed the planet, he arranged for corporations with good reputations to learn of the location of the planet, ensuring economic development for the Mluki while protecting them from unscrupulous exploitation.<ref name="COJ" />

==Powers and abilities==
As a Jedi Master, Plett was proficient in diverse Jedi arts. He was able to effect individuals' memories, which he did to the entire Mluki village living within Plett's Well, causing them to forget the presence of the Jedi within the valley. The effect of this persisted for over twelve years, and was described as preventing those Mluki from thinking about their past. The Jedi Master also created mental barriers using the Force that induced dread in those that approached. This was used to keep the Jedi children out of numerous caverns that contained the dangerous [[kretch insect]]. For even greater security, Plett sealed off passages using the Force. According to Mluki legends, the Ho'Din was also able to [[Animal bond|speak]] to birds and other creatures and affect the storms that periodically swept down the valleys. Plett was also skilled as a scientist; his genetic modifications he made to the plants in his valley thrived even decades after his departure.<ref name="COJ" />

==Equipment==
Plett frequently wore the black [[Jedi apparel/Legends|cloak of Jedi mastership]].<ref name="COJ" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:HoDin-UAA.jpg|thumb|right|upright|A possible image of Jedi Master Plett.]]
{{Quote|He'd been living here for some seventy years before the other Jedi brought their spouses and children here to hide.|Jevax|<ref name="COJ" />}}
Plett was created by [[Barbara Hambly]] for her novel [[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']] ([[1995]]). Written before the release of the prequel movies and their taboo on marriage, the "Children of the Jedi" were originally understood as spouses and children of Jedi Knights. However, ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' ([[2002]]), published after the prequel movies, [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] the children into [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]] from the Jedi Temple. A second post-prequel source makes oblique reference to Plett: [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 52]] has a story written from Plawal Dome on Belsavis. This is unusual in that the news story was written in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] when Plett was still on the planet.<ref name="HHN">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|52|sports/13418_2.html|Wan Approaches 50 in 50}}</ref> ''Children of the Jedi'' indicates that the name "Plett's Well" was corrupted to "Plawal" only after the Jedi had wiped the natives' memories and fled the planet.

The ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'', which lists Plett as a common Ho'Din name, depicts a yet-to-be-identified Ho'Din Jedi. Standing roughly 2.5 meters in height, with green skin<ref name="Skin">Not all Ho'Din have green skin; others are known to have [[Unidentified Ho'Din Jedi|blue]] or [[Spero|yellow]] pigmentation.</ref> and garbed in a black cloak, this image is consistent with the description of Plett given in ''Children of the Jedi''.
